commit.message:
Fix infinite loop in timer when interval_ms=0

When interval_ms is 0 (as fast as possible), the while loop
that advances the setpoint adds 0 each iteration, causing
an infinite loop. Guard the loop so it is skipped when
interval_ms is 0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Replace all Clients[x] NetClient access with Clients[x].connection
- Update all new NetClient[] allocations to new BlaeckClient[]
- Initialize identity fields on allocation and client connect
- Parse optional name/type from GET_DEVICES command
- Include client identity in disconnect log messages

commit.message:
Add Nx Release workflow documentation page (#1486)

This pull request updates the documentation to clarify and expand the
release automation options for monorepos, specifically by distinguishing
between Changesets and Nx-based release workflows. It introduces a new,
comprehensive guide for automating versioning and publishing with Nx,
and updates existing docs to direct Nx users to the appropriate
resource.

Resolves: CES-1451
depends-on: #1510

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Sf/fix deploy workflow (#83)

- [x] Fix remote command injection vulnerability in "Run deploy script"
step: move all user inputs to `env:`, base64-encode them before the
heredoc, decode on remote
- [x] Fix same vulnerability in "Sync repository on droplet" step
(DEPLOY_REF user input)
- [x] Code review and security scan passed

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: ubermensch01 <1215522+ubermensch01@users.noreply.github.com>

Enhance SSH navigation, root picker, and app settings (#333)

This pull request introduces a dedicated SSH server settings view and
refactors the SSH server editing experience for improved maintainability
and user experience. The main changes include adding a new route and UI
for SSH server settings, extracting and reusing form logic and fields,
and introducing a dedicated section for deleting SSH servers.

**New SSH Server Settings View and Routing:**

- Added a new route and navigation logic for an `"ssh-server-settings"`
view, allowing users to access and manage settings for individual SSH
servers. This includes updates to route parsing, navigation helpers, and
sidebar highlighting to support the new view.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-26ad4b834941d9b19ebf9db8082bd202aaf72ea0ddea85f5a8a0cb3c729cc6f2R71-R77)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-26ad4b834941d9b19ebf9db8082bd202aaf72ea0ddea85f5a8a0cb3c729cc6f2R163-R165)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-33ff5a804d18cd25d070b975420c3b030b62c7cdf2850370785969ddadc24f44R53)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-e7a1387c2bd5293a16be1756082eb79da3365f2d3140ea577b0167ad248bb55eL183-R186)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-d5434fd5e0a45d3e881492b71f40257e5c207afed228b6603ec70070c4894eb5L244-R249)

**Refactoring and Reuse of SSH Server Form Logic:**

- Extracted SSH server form state and logic into utility functions and a
dedicated `SshServerFields` component, improving code reuse and
maintainability between the composer and settings views. This includes
new helpers for value creation, trimming, and update request building.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-9d14e151b29c02e9c7d75e3c76ff8dc832a8612acb0ec89506f8136bd89feb15L4-R13)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-9d14e151b29c02e9c7d75e3c76ff8dc832a8612acb0ec89506f8136bd89feb15L30-R41)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-9d14e151b29c02e9c7d75e3c76ff8dc832a8612acb0ec89506f8136bd89feb15R50-R68)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-9d14e151b29c02e9c7d75e3c76ff8dc832a8612acb0ec89506f8136bd89feb15L84-R92)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-9d14e151b29c02e9c7d75e3c76ff8dc832a8612acb0ec89506f8136bd89feb15L139-L167)
[[6]](diffhunk://#diff-eba82210d4d0f47ef27e72de08bd63b6f3362ed0d4e15feb13e5606a0f71811cR1-R81)
[[7]](diffhunk://#diff-35e4fff0e710e53219fb8c863b3fbbe630b84a02b098c5eb77f1f2175a38cd33R1-R54)

**Dedicated Delete SSH Server Section:**

- Added a new `DeleteSshServerSection` component, providing a clear and
safe UI for deleting SSH servers, including confirmation and error
handling. This section is now part of the SSH server settings view.

**UI and Component Integration:**

- Integrated the new SSH server settings view into the main shell
content rendering logic, ensuring correct display and fallback when a
server is missing.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-51ff8bc1d91b5f3294a292aa8af67582d8f5f3301d4d822e135ef5832bb86fe3R19)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-51ff8bc1d91b5f3294a292aa8af67582d8f5f3301d4d822e135ef5832bb86fe3L141)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-51ff8bc1d91b5f3294a292aa8af67582d8f5f3301d4d822e135ef5832bb86fe3L352-R379)

These changes collectively improve the modularity, clarity, and user
experience of SSH server management in the application.

commit.message:
feat: Add ONNX embedding backend and RRF fusion

- Implemented ONNX Runtime CPU-optimized embedding backend
- Added Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) for blended sparse+dense retrieval
- ONNX backend with graceful fallback to hash embeddings
- RRF provides deterministic fusion of BM25 and vector search results
- Added onnxruntime and tokenizers to optional dependencies
- Configured RRF with k=60 (industry standard)
- ONNX uses mean pooling and L2 normalization

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: token-reducer
repository.description: ⚡ Cut Claude token usage by 90%+ — free, open-source, local-first context compression for Claude Code. Hybrid RAG (BM25 + ONNX vectors), AST chunking, reranking. No API needed.

feat: multi-token registration, audit logging, host group management refinements

Phase 2 refinements based on user feedback:

- Multi-token system: RegistrationToken entity with TTL, labels, individual
  revocation. Host groups now support multiple active tokens.
- Host group deletion: type-to-confirm (AWS-style) with cascade delete of
  hosts and tokens. All actions audit-logged.
- Audit logging: HostGroup.Created/Deleted, RegistrationToken.Generated/
  Revoked, Host.Added/Removed — all recorded with username and timestamp.
- Docker compose: workflowhost containers depend on webapi healthy state;
  WorkflowHost startup has 5-attempt retry with exponential backoff.
- Keycloak: added demo_admin user (password: webapi) with admin role.
- DB provisioning: scripts for Linux (.sh) and Windows (.ps1) to set up
  job databases for new host groups.
- Frontend: tabbed host group cards (Hosts/Tokens/Activity), host group
  detail page with jobs tab and host-level job history, token management
  with label/TTL, delete confirmation modal.
- Documentation: comprehensive HOSTGROUPS.md update covering multi-token
  architecture, audit logging, deletion workflow, and provisioning scripts.
- Tests: 124 passing (24 new tests for multi-token system).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add US-5.15/5.16/5.17 — output event streams, Observable ViewModel, CounterState async forwarding

- US-5.15: AsyncStream emission for no-action -*> output events; stream lifecycle,
  emitX in runtime, fromRuntime bridge, self-transition stream renewal
- US-5.16: Optional @Observable ViewModel generation (generateViewModel config flag),
  bridges AsyncStream → @Observable for SwiftUI, kept separate to avoid platform floor
- US-5.17: async withX variants on XxxState, direct in-place method forwarding
  (state.increment()), outputs property forwarding (nil when wrong phase)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add local LLM fine-tuning pipeline (mlx-lm + LFM2.5-1.2B)

- llm/generate_training_data.py: extracts 77 chat JSONL pairs from Scala 3 source
- llm/train.sh: LoRA fine-tuning via mlx_lm.lora (200 iters, 4 layers, batch 4)
- llm/serve.sh: OpenAI-compatible server on port 8080 (fused or adapter mode)
- llm/baseline.sh: before/after comparison prompts
- llm/continue_config.json: Continue.dev config for VS Code IDE integration
- llm/.gitignore: excludes venv, nanochat, adapters, fused model, data

Validated run on M3 24GB:
- Model: LiquidAI/LFM2.5-1.2B-Instruct-MLX-4bit (663 MB)
- Training: loss 3.13 -> 0.065 in 200 iters (~12 min)
- Peak RAM: 3.65 GB (leaves 20 GB free for backend + IDE)
- Inference: 105 tok/s with adapters loaded

Next: expand training data to 500+ pairs, then graduate to Qwen2.5-Coder-14B

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(explorer): support custom roots for workspace and server files (#329)

This pull request introduces a new feature that allows users to set a
custom root directory for the file explorer in both workspace and server
views. It also updates the backend APIs to support this feature and
improves error handling related to directory navigation. The most
important changes are grouped below:

**Frontend: File Explorer Root Directory Customization**

* Added a UI form in `FileExplorerView` to allow users to set or reset
the explorer root directory, with the current and default root shown and
input validation handled. The explorer now navigates based on this root
directory. (`src/components/app-shell/file-explorer-view.tsx`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-53e454912dbb99b25035e088617f0f1e82d2f4928e1eb78618033e8252a245edR35-R40)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-53e454912dbb99b25035e088617f0f1e82d2f4928e1eb78618033e8252a245edR52)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-53e454912dbb99b25035e088617f0f1e82d2f4928e1eb78618033e8252a245edR70-R71)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-53e454912dbb99b25035e088617f0f1e82d2f4928e1eb78618033e8252a245edR86-R89)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-53e454912dbb99b25035e088617f0f1e82d2f4928e1eb78618033e8252a245edR120-R121)
[[6]](diffhunk://#diff-53e454912dbb99b25035e088617f0f1e82d2f4928e1eb78618033e8252a245edR130-R157)
[[7]](diffhunk://#diff-53e454912dbb99b25035e088617f0f1e82d2f4928e1eb78618033e8252a245edR171-R217)
[[8]](diffhunk://#diff-53e454912dbb99b25035e088617f0f1e82d2f4928e1eb78618033e8252a245edR342)
* Updated routing and navigation logic in `App.tsx` to parse and build
URLs with the `startDirectory` parameter, ensuring the explorer root is
preserved across navigation and deep links. (`src/App.tsx`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-26ad4b834941d9b19ebf9db8082bd202aaf72ea0ddea85f5a8a0cb3c729cc6f2R10-R20)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-26ad4b834941d9b19ebf9db8082bd202aaf72ea0ddea85f5a8a0cb3c729cc6f2L58-R67)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-26ad4b834941d9b19ebf9db8082bd202aaf72ea0ddea85f5a8a0cb3c729cc6f2L79-R88)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-26ad4b834941d9b19ebf9db8082bd202aaf72ea0ddea85f5a8a0cb3c729cc6f2R119-R129)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-26ad4b834941d9b19ebf9db8082bd202aaf72ea0ddea85f5a8a0cb3c729cc6f2L128-R148)
[[6]](diffhunk://#diff-26ad4b834941d9b19ebf9db8082bd202aaf72ea0ddea85f5a8a0cb3c729cc6f2L137-R157)

**Backend: API Support for Custom Explorer Roots**

* Modified workspace and SSH server files API endpoints to accept an
optional `startDirectory` parameter and use it as the explorer root,
including passing it through to file operations.
(`src/api/ssh-server-files.ts`, `src/api/workspaces/files.ts`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b06f85206337c881984a328e191010349c0a9b78d0fa89112c9f79d7ddd0b8dcL6-R10)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b06f85206337c881984a328e191010349c0a9b78d0fa89112c9f79d7ddd0b8dcL66-R79)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-b06f85206337c881984a328e191010349c0a9b78d0fa89112c9f79d7ddd0b8dcR92-R100)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-b06f85206337c881984a328e191010349c0a9b78d0fa89112c9f79d7ddd0b8dcL97-R114)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-b06f85206337c881984a328e191010349c0a9b78d0fa89112c9f79d7ddd0b8dcL127-R144)
[[6]](diffhunk://#diff-b06f85206337c881984a328e191010349c0a9b78d0fa89112c9f79d7ddd0b8dcL153-R170)
[[7]](diffhunk://#diff-b06f85206337c881984a328e191010349c0a9b78d0fa89112c9f79d7ddd0b8dcL181-R198)
[[8]](diffhunk://#diff-783937001d899110c074b41451df61a8b3bf87ef34181fae5be5adb2b993e7dfL74-R83)
[[9]](diffhunk://#diff-783937001d899110c074b41451df61a8b3bf87ef34181fae5be5adb2b993e7dfL100-R111)
[[10]](diffhunk://#diff-783937001d899110c074b41451df61a8b3bf87ef34181fae5be5adb2b993e7dfL125-R138)
[[11]](diffhunk://#diff-783937001d899110c074b41451df61a8b3bf87ef34181fae5be5adb2b993e7dfR177)

**Error Handling Improvements**

* Enhanced error mapping for both workspace and server file APIs to
provide specific error codes and messages for invalid or non-existent
start directories, and updated error messages to reflect the new concept
of an "active explorer root". (`src/api/ssh-server-files.ts`,
`src/api/workspaces/files.ts`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b06f85206337c881984a328e191010349c0a9b78d0fa89112c9f79d7ddd0b8dcL35-R54)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-783937001d899110c074b41451df61a8b3bf87ef34181fae5be5adb2b993e7dfR32-R44)

These changes together enable users to flexibly set the starting point
for file exploration, improve navigation consistency, and provide
clearer error feedback when navigating outside allowed directories.

---------

Co-authored-by: Pablo Zaidenvoren <pablozaiden@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add host management and agent registration system

Implement a self-hosted agent/runner pattern for FlowForge, allowing
WorkflowHost agents to be deployed on any machine and self-register
with the platform using secure registration tokens.

Domain:
- Add RegistrationTokenHash to HostGroup entity with SHA-256 hashing
- Add GenerateRegistrationToken, ValidateRegistrationToken, RevokeRegistrationToken methods
- Add domain exceptions: HostGroupNotFound, InvalidRegistrationToken, HostNotFound
- EF Core migration for RegistrationTokenHash column

Backend API:
- POST /api/host-groups/{id}/registration-token - generate token (admin)
- DELETE /api/host-groups/{id}/registration-token - revoke token (admin)
- POST /api/host-groups/{id}/hosts - manually add host (admin)
- DELETE /api/host-groups/{id}/hosts/{hostId} - remove host (admin)
- GET /api/host-groups/{id}/hosts - list hosts with status
- POST /api/hosts/register - token-based agent self-registration (no JWT)

WorkflowHost self-registration:
- Three registration modes: HOST_GROUP_ID, REGISTRATION_TOKEN, HOST_CONNECTION_ID
- Automatic registration on startup with upsert semantics
- Docker compose updated with HOST_CONNECTION_ID env vars

Frontend:
- Enhanced HostGroups page with token generation, host CRUD, setup instructions
- Token copy-once modal with security warning
- Auto-refreshing host status (15s polling)
- Setup guides for Docker, Linux systemd, and Windows service

Documentation:
- New docs/HOSTGROUPS.md with comprehensive deployment guide
- Covers Docker, Linux, Windows, cloud VM deployment options
- API reference, network requirements, troubleshooting

Tests:
- 16 new unit tests for HostGroup token system (all 117 pass)
- End-to-end verified via docker compose

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(deploy): make nginx listen on Railway's dynamic PORT via envsubst

Railway injects PORT env var — nginx must listen on it instead of hardcoded 80.
Use envsubst to replace \ in nginx template at container startup.

commit.message:
Align CMK/encryption articles with authoritative Q&A guidance

- Update FIPS 140-2 references to FIPS 140-3
- Remove CMK from AKV Standard use cases (Premium minimum)
- Remove retired services from CMK support list (LUIS, Personalizer, QnA Maker, MySQL Single Server, StorSimple)
- Add CMK control model framing to CMK support list intro
- Clarify key rotation only re-wraps DEKs, not re-encrypts data
- Add RSA-OAEP-256 wrapping algorithm recommendation
- Add KV Premium minimum and MHSM sovereignty guidance
- Clarify double encryption uses independent keys/operators/hierarchies
- Add security domain ownership and irrecoverable loss warning for MHSM
- Fix HSM typo (Model -> Module) in secrets-best-practices
